name: GPT-5.5 (High)
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); codename "Spud"; 'high' reasoning-effort configuration
note: Reasoning-effort sibling of the already-catalogued base 'GPT-5.5' entry. Same weights, same pricing/context as the default/xhigh siblings; distinguished only by the reasoning.effort=high API parameter (also selectable in the ChatGPT model picker's effort selector). Same-family, same-release-date, same-paramsB per the effort-tier convention.

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam (no tools), from Artificial Analysis's per-model JSON data payload keyed to slug "gpt-5-5-high" (id b13c1257-d746-4027-8fc8-4892dc14701c), confirmed distinct from the sibling 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' and 'GPT-5.5 (medium)' entries on the same site.
IFBench, from Artificial Analysis's per-model data for 'GPT-5.5 (high)'.
GDPval-AA v2, normalized score (gdpvalNormalized field), from Artificial Analysis's per-model data for 'GPT-5.5 (high)'.
MMMU-Pro, from Artificial Analysis's per-model data for 'GPT-5.5 (high)'.
Terminal-Bench v2.1 (field 'terminalbenchV21'), from Artificial Analysis's per-model data for 'GPT-5.5 (high)'.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Codex harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 24.2% / Score 44.0% (rank 13). Matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel ('High'). Score component used per corpus convention.

verificationNotes:
OpenAI's own reasoning-models guide and OpenAI Help Center confirm GPT-5.5 exposes none/low/medium/high/xhigh reasoning-effort levels, with Plus-tier ChatGPT users able to select 'High' directly in the model picker. Artificial Analysis independently benchmarks this exact configuration as a distinct model page ('GPT-5.5 (high)') with its own composite score (53, rank #12/188), separate from both the base/medium config already in the corpus and the 'xhigh' page below (55, rank #7/188) — confirming these are genuinely differentiated, independently-measured configurations rather than a single number relabeled three ways. Could not find high-tier-specific per-benchmark (GPQA/AIME/etc.) scores published separately from the flagship figures already recorded under the base 'GPT-5.5' entry, which appear (per exact-match FrontierMath figures) to actually represent the xhigh configuration — flagging this for the ingesting pipeline; recommend also flipping the existing 'GPT-5.5' entry's isBestConfig to false now that a genuine xhigh sibling is being added.

2026-07-16: Resolved the 8 flagged core-benchmark gaps for GPT-5.5 (High) via live research across three independent source types. ADDED gpqa=93.2 from Artificial Analysis's dedicated GPQA Diamond evaluation leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/gpqa-diamond), which explicitly lists 'GPT-5.5 (high)' as a distinct data point from both the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' sibling (93.5, already on that entry) and the OpenAI-self-reported xhigh-mislabeled figure (93.6) already on the base 'GPT-5.5' entry. The remaining seven (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu) were checked and found to be genuine absences for the 'high' configuration specifically: (1) OpenAI's own 'Introducing GPT-5.5' launch page's evaluation tables cover only Terminal-Bench 2.0, SWE-Bench Pro, GDPval, BrowseComp, MCP Atlas, GPQA Diamond, FrontierMath T1-3/T4, HLE, OSWorld-Verified, Tau2-bench Telecom, and ARC-AGI-2 -- none of the seven legacy/classic benchmarks appear at all, for any reasoning-effort tier. (2) Artificial Analysis's 'GPT-5.5 (high)' model page tracks 18 evaluations (GDPval-AA v2, Tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience Accuracy/Non-Hallucination, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, AutomationBench-AA, Harvey LAB-AA, EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA, IFBench, APEX-Agents-AA, ITBench-AA, MMMU-Pro) -- again none of MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, or plain MMMU (only MMMU-Pro, already tracked). (3) Epoch AI's GPT-5.5 model page (epoch.ai/models/gpt-5-5) has a reasoning-tier selector that was switched live via browser interaction specifically to 'GPT-5.5 (high)' -- at that exact tier, Epoch has run evaluations only for Surface Evolver Bench, WeirdML, DeepResearchBench (agents); DeepSWE, CursorBench, SciCode, Text Arena (coding); CritPt (math/science); and ARC-AGI-1/2, CL-bench Life, BTF-3 (misc). Notably, OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025, GPQA Diamond, FrontierMath, and SWE-bench Verified appear on Epoch's page only under other reasoning-effort-tier selections (i.e. not 'high'), confirming those specific evals were run at a different tier (evidently xhigh) for this model family. Also checked Vals.ai: its single 'GPT 5.5' model page's Hyperparameter-settings panel explicitly states Reasoning Effort = xhigh (confirmed by expanding the panel live), so its GPQA Diamond/LiveCodeBench/MMLU-Pro/MMMU/SWE-bench figures belong to the xhigh sibling entry, not this 'high' entry, and were correctly excluded here to avoid mislabeling. Conclusion: these seven omissions are consistent with (a) the well-established frontier-lab pattern of retiring saturated legacy benchmarks (MMLU, MATH-500, HumanEval) in favor of newer suites, already noted elsewhere in this corpus, and (b) the narrower fact that even benchmarks still actively run per-effort-tier (GPQA Diamond, AIME, SWE-bench Verified) were, per Epoch AI's own tier-by-tier breakdown, evaluated by vendors only at the xhigh and/or best-available tiers for GPT-5.5, not at 'high' specifically -- a real, citable absence rather than a research gap.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep pass, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Re-chased all four gaps through six previously-unexhausted source types, deliberately avoiding the launch page / system card / Epoch AI / Vals.ai / LMArena / BenchLM / llm-stats.com sources the first pass already exhausted. No qualifying score found for any of the four benchmarks; all four remain omitted_no_evidence.

(1) Stanford HELM -- loaded live in-browser (not static fetch, since HELM's SPA doesn't render via plain HTML fetch): HELM Classic 'MMLU' leaderboard, HELM 'Lite' leaderboard (which does carry classic MMLU-EM and MATH-CoT columns), and HELM 'Capabilities' leaderboard (MMLU-Pro/GPQA/IFEval/WildBench/Omni-MATH). None of the three has ever run GPT-5.5 at all -- the most recent model present on any of them is GPT-5.1 (2025-11-13) on Capabilities; Lite and Classic MMLU top out even earlier (GPT-4o/DeepSeek-v3/Claude 3.5 era). HELM simply has not benchmarked this model family yet, for any reasoning-effort tier -- a real absence, not a search failure.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar: multiple targeted searches ("GPT-5.5 MMLU site:arxiv.org", "GPT-5.5 AIME 2025 site:semanticscholar.org OR site:arxiv.org", "arxiv.org gpt-5.5 evaluation table") turned up only GPT-5(.0) medical/radiology papers and the MathArena paper (arXiv:2605.00674) -- fetched its abstract directly: it reports GPT-5.5 (unqualified, i.e. the vendor-run xhigh config per MathArena's own site labeling, see #4 below) at 98% on a 2026 USAMO-style set and 74% on research-level questions, but that is neither a MATH-500 nor an AIME number and has no 'high'-tier-specific breakdown. No arXiv/Semantic Scholar paper reports plain MMLU, MATH-500, real AIME, or HumanEval for any GPT-5.5 configuration.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs: searched EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness and related repos for GPT-5.5 results.json artifacts; found only harness infrastructure/support-for-GPT-5-family commits, no committed run output against GPT-5.5 (any tier).

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: (a) OpenRouter's openai/gpt-5.5 page (rendered live) has a 'Benchmarks' section header but no populated MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval scores, and doesn't expose a 'high' vs 'xhigh' distinction at all. (b) LiveBench.ai (rendered live) does track a distinct 'GPT-5.5 Thinking High Effort' row (overall 78.8, separate from 'GPT-5.5 Thinking xHigh Effort' at 79.9) -- confirming independent trackers do distinguish this exact 'High' config -- but LiveBench's seven categories (Reasoning/Coding/Agentic Coding/Mathematics/Data Analysis/Language/Instruction Following) are LiveBench's own contamination-resistant proprietary problem sets, not the classic MMLU/MATH-500/AIME/HumanEval instruments, so no score maps onto this corpus's benchmark taxonomy. (c) MathArena.ai (ETH Zurich SRI Lab, arXiv:2605.00674, live GitHub+HuggingFace data) publishes a dedicated, 61-model AIME-2025 leaderboard -- checked it directly (not just the overall/cross-competition summary): GPT-5, GPT-5.1, and GPT-5.2 all appear with explicit '(high)' tier rows, but GPT-5.5 does not appear on the AIME-2025 leaderboard in any tier; GPT-5.5 only appears elsewhere on MathArena's site as 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' in the cross-competition 'Expected performance' ranking (81.2%). This is strong, direct, independent confirmation that no organization -- vendor or academic -- has published a real AIME-2025 score for the 'high' reasoning-effort tier specifically. PapersWithCode and Scale SEAL were also checked via search with no GPT-5.5 hits at all.

(5) Non-English regional sources: searched with Chinese/Japanese terms mixed into the query; search tooling returned only English-language results (predominantly uncited SEO/content-farm sites, see below), no genuine Chinese/Korean/Japanese community blog or forum post with a citable GPT-5.5 (high) score surfaced.

(6) Sibling models: re-read both sibling corpus files in full -- corpus/models/openai/gpt-5-5.json (base) and corpus/models/openai/gpt-5-5-xhigh.json (xHigh) -- both already contain exhaustive, dated (2026-07-16) gap-resolution passes for these exact four benchmarks (plus livecodebench/swe-bench/mmmu) covering OpenAI's own materials, Artificial Analysis, Epoch AI (including raw benchmarks.csv/external_benchmarks CSV exports, which 404 for mmlu.csv/math.csv/humaneval.csv), and Vals.ai, concluding a family-wide, tier-independent absence of MMLU/MATH-500/AIME/HumanEval for GPT-5.5 at any reasoning-effort level. No sibling entry's comparison table contains a 'GPT-5.5 (high)' MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval data point either.

Also independently re-confirmed and rejected as unusable (consistent with both sibling files' own prior rejections): automatio.ai, gpt0x.com, promptchief.tech, tokenmix.ai, enter.converge.ai, ai.cc, o-mega.ai, llmreference.com, kingy.ai (for these specific numbers), and startupfortune.com all publish MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figures for 'GPT-5.5' with zero citations, no reasoning-effort-tier qualification, and numbers that contradict each other across sites (MMLU quoted as 88.1%, 92.4%, 92.5%; AIME 2025 as 81.2% and 100%; HumanEval as 94.2%) -- classic uncited SEO-farm content, not used for any addition.

Conclusion: mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval remain genuine, well-attested absences for GPT-5.5 (High) specifically -- and, per the sibling files' parallel deep passes, for the entire GPT-5.5 family at every reasoning-effort tier -- rather than a research gap. All four gaps are proposed as omitted_no_evidence.

2026-07-18 (round-3 final pass, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Re-chased all four remaining gaps through five genuinely-attempted new source types per the round-3 brief, deliberately avoiding every source already exhausted in rounds 1-2 (official launch materials/system card, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com live pages, Stanford HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, EleutherAI harness, MathArena, LiveBench, OpenRouter, Scale SEAL, regional-language search, sibling-model files). No qualifying score surfaced for any of the four benchmarks; all four remain omitted_no_evidence.

(1) Wayback Machine -- genuinely attempted at length but ultimately INCONCLUSIVE due to a structural network block, not a clean negative. Using archive.org's CDX/'available' API (which did respond), confirmed real archived snapshots exist: OpenAI's launch page 'introducing-gpt-5-5' at 2026-04-25T17:46:12Z and 2026-05-12T05:01:36Z; Artificial Analysis's 'gpt-5-5-high' model page at 2026-04-23T21:00:18Z (release day itself); llm-stats.com's 'gpt-5.5' page at 2026-04-24T13:16:35Z and 2026-05-17T18:32:55Z. However, every attempt to actually retrieve the archived HTML content of these snapshots -- direct curl, varied browser User-Agent/Accept headers, a dedicated 15-attempt background retry loop spaced 25s apart over ~6 minutes, and an independent third-party fetch proxy (r.jina.ai) -- returned HTTP 429 from web.archive.org every single time (the one proxy service gave an explicit reason: this environment's network ASN is globally rate-limited/flagged for 'bad network reputation', confirming this is an environment-level block rather than a transient single failure or a sign that the content itself is unavailable). Could not compare live-vs-archived benchmark tables as instructed. This source is marked genuinely-attempted-but-blocked, not a clean check.

(2) Conference talks / keynote transcripts -- OpenAI DevDay 2026 is scheduled for 2026-09-29 (per devday.openai.com), i.e. after both GPT-5.5's 2026-04-23 release and today's date (2026-07-18) -- no DevDay keynote covering GPT-5.5 exists yet to check. Searched available OpenAI podcast/YouTube content instead (the OpenAI Podcast's Sam Altman AGI/GPT-5 episode, Lex Fridman's Sam Altman interview, 'OpenAI Dev Day in 4 Minutes' recap): none contain a verbally-stated or slide-shown MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure for GPT-5.5 specifically (any tier).

(3) Investor / press materials -- OpenAI is privately held and does not file SEC documents (confirmed no 10-Q/10-K/8-K exists under OpenAI's name). Checked Microsoft's FY2026 Q1/Q2/Q3 earnings call transcripts (via Motley Fool, Intellectia, and microsoft.com/investor) and adjacent investor-facing content (Nadella's Latent Space/No Priors crossover, Stratechery interview, Morgan Stanley TMT conference, Fortune coverage of Nadella's June 2026 SF trip): GPT-5.5 is referenced qualitatively (available via Azure/AWS, used in an internal distillation demo, GPT-5.2 cited by name for Maia-silicon throughput) but no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure for GPT-5.5 appears in any earnings-call transcript or investor deck.

(4) GitHub issues / SDK repo discussions -- searched both Issues (GitHub REST search API, org-wide 'org:openai' plus per-repo) and Discussions (GraphQL discussion search, both full-text and most-recent-N per repo) across openai/openai-python, openai/openai-node, openai/openai-cookbook, openai/openai-agents-python, openai/codex, openai/simple-evals, and openai/evals for 'GPT-5.5' combined with MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Zero qualifying hits in any combination -- only unrelated model-support PRs (e.g. adding gpt-5.5/gpt-5.6 to model-default lists), performance/latency complaints (openai/codex #22005, #28885, #30539), and context-window bug reports turned up; no maintainer or OpenAI engineer posted a benchmark figure for this model.

(5) Third-party review/analysis sites not yet checked this session -- Simon Willison (simonwillison.net main site + Mastodon feed), Nathan Lambert/interconnects.ai, Ethan Mollick/oneusefulthing.org ('Sign of the future: GPT-5.5' -- confirmed it covers only qualitative capability demos, e.g. GPT-5.5 Pro finishing a 3D-harbor-town coding task in 20min vs GPT-5.4 Pro's 33min, no formal benchmark table at all), SemiAnalysis newsletter (covers Codex coding-workflow impressions, no benchmark figures), ChinaTalk (no GPT-5.5-specific benchmark article), DataCamp's GPT-5.5 writeup, and Vellum.ai's GPT-5.5 writeup were all fetched and checked directly: none report MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval for GPT-5.5 -- DataCamp and Vellum's own comparison tables list only the modern suite (SWE-Bench Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.0/2.1, GDPval, GPQA Diamond, FrontierMath, HLE, OSWorld-Verified, BrowseComp, MCP Atlas, CyberGym, ARC-AGI-2, MRCR v2, Tau2-bench), consistent with the corpus's existing finding that these four legacy benchmarks were never published by OpenAI for GPT-5.5 at any tier. Also re-surfaced and re-rejected (same recycled, mutually-contradictory, uncited figures already flagged in the round-2 notes) automatio.ai, gpt0x.com, promptchief.tech, tokenmix.ai, and kingy.ai -- e.g. tokenmix.ai's 'GPT-5.5 Spud Review' still quotes 92.4% MMLU while automatio.ai/gpt0x.com quote 88.1% MMLU-Pro and 94.2% HumanEval elsewhere, with zero primary-source citations anywhere -- not used.

Conclusion: 4 of the 5 mandated new source types (conference talks, investor/press materials, GitHub issues/discussions, third-party analysis sites) produced clean, well-documented, genuine negatives; the 5th (Wayback Machine) was genuinely and extensively attempted (confirmed real snapshots exist at the right dates via the CDX/available API) but content retrieval was blocked by a structural, environment-level network rate-limit rather than yielding a positive or negative content result. Combined with the two prior independent research rounds' exhaustive negatives across a dozen-plus other source types, this constitutes a third consecutive absence across three independent search strategies -- strong, durable evidence that mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval are genuine, permanent omissions for GPT-5.5 (High), consistent with the well-documented frontier-lab pattern of retiring these saturated legacy benchmarks. All four gaps remain proposed as omitted_no_evidence; no further research rounds are expected to change this conclusion absent OpenAI or a major independent tracker publishing new legacy-benchmark data for this model family.
